band.name: A Boy Called Hero
band.tagline: Turn it up until your neighbours hate you.

bio.elevator: A Boy Called Hero is a UK pop-punk duo channeling the raw energy and melodic hooks of the early 2000s into anthems for right now.
bio.short:
Twenty years ago, Richie and Sam met through a mutual friend and clicked almost instantly.

They were into the same music, laughed at the same stupid jokes, and seemed to understand each other in that rare, effortless way that only happens a few times in life. Before long, the friendship became creative, and the two started a little music project called Stars of Orion.

They began writing straight away.

Like many young bands, Stars of Orion never quite became what they imagined it might. Life moved on, the project faded, and the songs were packed away onto Richie’s hard drive, where they sat quietly for the next twenty years.

But the music never really disappeared.

Two decades later, Richie revisited those old ideas and found that something was still there. The songs weren’t the same anymore, and neither were they. The album Richie and Sam are now writing does not simply revive those early tracks, but carries pieces of them forward: melodies, feelings, fragments, and the spirit of two friends making music because they had to.

Over the years, Richie and Sam have been in and out of each other’s pockets, getting each other through the hard times, the good times, and everything in between. That friendship is the heart of A Boy Called Hero.

Now, after twenty years of stories, songs, false starts, late nights, and unfinished ideas, they are deep into writing and recording their first album.

And boy, is it going to be a good one.

bio.long:
Twenty years ago, Richie and Sam met through a mutual friend and clicked almost instantly.

They were into the same music, laughed at the same stupid jokes, and seemed to understand each other in that rare, effortless way that only happens a few times in life. Before long, the friendship became creative, and the two started a little music project called Stars of Orion.

They began writing straight away.

Like many young bands, Stars of Orion never quite became what they imagined it might. Life moved on, the project faded, and the songs were packed away onto Richie’s hard drive, where they sat quietly for the next twenty years.

But the music never really disappeared.

Two decades later, Richie revisited those old ideas and found that something was still there. The songs weren’t the same anymore, and neither were they. The album Richie and Sam are now writing does not simply revive those early tracks, but carries pieces of them forward: melodies, feelings, fragments, and the spirit of two friends making music because they had to.

Over the years, Richie and Sam have been in and out of each other’s pockets, getting each other through the hard times, the good times, and everything in between. That friendship is the heart of A Boy Called Hero.

Now, after twenty years of stories, songs, false starts, late nights, and unfinished ideas, they are deep into writing and recording their first album.

And boy, is it going to be a good one.

releases.title: The Sound of the End
releases.description: The debut single. A anthemic blast of driving guitars, crashing drums, and hooks that refuse to leave your head.
releases.lyrics:
Verse 1:

Insert a metaphor
Sprinkle on a simile
Ask what this life is for
Top it with a melody
Sing about an ex
How bad they hurt me
Tie it in a bow
In a break up story

Chorus:

I can't forget
The words she said
You hold me down
And I've got to let you go
I can't forget
The words she said
You hold me down
This is the Sound of the End

Verse 2:

A catchy hook
A small piano piece
She's got me shook
I need a symphony
I heard a sound
A rhythm of a timpani
I wrote it down
And this is what she said to me

Chorus:

I can't forget
The words she said
You hold me down
And I've got to let you go
I can't forget
The words she said
You hold me down
This is the Sound of the End
releases.meaning: "The Sound of the End" is very meta, a song about how to write a breakup song, whilst being a breakup song itself. The Sound of the End is the echo of a final conversation, the memory of painful words, and the act of turning heartbreak into music. It captures that strange place where pain becomes art: when the only way to survive the ending is to sing about it.
